name: Pointer Reef
description: Pointer Reef sits off New Providence in the Bahamas, where the Atlantic meets the Caribbean. The site features dramatic coral formations, swim-throughs, and steep drop-offs teeming with marine life. Expect schools of jacks, reef sharks, and colorful tropical fish. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with depths ranging from 12 to 30 meters. The vibrant coral and clear waters create a stunning underwater landscape. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to the currents.

name: Train Wreck
description: Explore a sunken train resting in shallow turquoise waters, surrounded by vibrant coral and schools of tropical fish. The wreck sits intact at 30 feet, making it ideal for beginners and photographers. Eagle rays and nurse sharks often glide past the rusting cars, while colorful sponges and sea fans cover the structure. Strong currents can sweep through, but visibility remains excellent. A surreal underwater adventure blending history and marine life.

name: Etheridge
description: Etheridge sits off Grand Bahama in the Atlantic, where clear waters reveal dramatic coral formations and swim-throughs. Schools of tropical fish dart around vibrant reefs, while eagle rays and reef sharks occasionally glide past. The site offers gentle currents, making it ideal for divers of all levels. Depths range from shallow coral gardens to deeper walls, ensuring variety. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet, showcasing the thriving marine ecosystem. A great spot for both photography and experienced divers seeking encounters with larger pelagics.

name: Picasso's Gallery
description: This vibrant reef in the Bahamas dazzles with colorful sponges, sea fans, and schools of tropical fish. The shallow depth and gentle current make it ideal for beginners. Look for parrotfish, angelfish, and the occasional nurse shark gliding past. The coral formations create swim-throughs and overhangs perfect for exploration. Crystal-clear visibility and warm water enhance the experience. A must-dive for underwater photographers.

name: Ugly Duckling
description: This site off Grand Bahama features a stunning coral-covered wreck resting at 60 feet. Schools of tropical fish swarm the structure, while reef sharks and turtles often pass by. The clear Caribbean waters offer excellent visibility, making it ideal for photographers. The wreck's intact hull and swim-throughs create an exciting exploration. Gentle currents suit divers of all levels. Nearby reefs add to the marine life encounters.

name: Washing Machine
region: The Caribbean, Bahamas, The Exuma Cays

name: The Strip
description: This site features a dramatic underwater ridge teeming with marine life. The steep drop-off plunges to over 100 feet, attracting reef sharks, eagle rays, and large schools of jacks. Coral formations cling to the wall while colorful sponges dot the sandy bottom. Stronger currents bring pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet in these clear Bahamian waters. The shallow plateau offers shelter for nurse sharks and turtles. Best dived on incoming tide when conditions are calmest.
